requirements.text: WHEN a subdocument request contains '..' or path traversal sequences in subDocName, the system shall reject the request with 404 status
WHEN a subdocument request contains a valid subDocName, the system shall return the subdocument content unchanged
WHEN a project config specifies ticketsPath containing '..' segments, the system shall reject the configuration at load time
WHEN a project config specifies a simple relative ticketsPath like 'docs/CRs', the system shall accept it
WHEN a project config specifies an absolute ticketsPath like '/var/mdt/tickets', the system shall accept it
WHEN a symlink exists inside ticketDir and allowSymlinks is false or absent, the system shall not follow the symlink
WHEN allowSymlinks is true and a symlink target resolves inside ticketDir, the system shall follow the symlink and serve content
WHEN allowSymlinks is true and a symlink target resolves outside ticketDir, the system shall reject the request
Path validation overhead shall be less than 1ms per request
No regression in existing subdocument test suite
API contract unchanged - same endpoints and same responses for valid requests
Null byte injection via percent-encoded null (%00) in subDocName shall be rejected
SubDocName exceeding 255 characters shall be rejected
SubDocName containing '..' segments (literal dotdot, e.g. '../etc/passwd') shall be rejected at input validation before any path resolution
requirements.sourceRef: CR Section 4: AC 1,2 + Section 3: Express decodes %2F

requirements.text: Double encoding via %252F in subDocName shall be rejected
Unicode slash variants in subDocName shall be rejected

WHEN a project config specifies an absolute ticketsPath that is exactly equal to a protected system root directory (e.g. /etc, /usr, C:\\Windows), the system shall reject the configuration at load time with message: Choose a subfolder, not a system root directory.
Absolute ticketsPath that is a subdirectory of a protected root (e.g. /usr/local/my-project) shall be accepted

scenarios.title: Valid subdocument is returned unchanged
scenarios.given: a project with CR MDT-151 exists with a subdocument named 'architecture'
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent to /api/projects/{id}/crs/MDT-151/subdocuments/architecture
scenarios.then: the response status is 200 and the subdocument content is returned

scenarios.title: Config rejects ticketsPath with dotdot segments
scenarios.given: a project directory without a configured ticketsPath
scenarios.when: the project config is loaded with ticketsPath set to '../../shared'
scenarios.then: the configuration is rejected with a validation error

scenarios.title: Config accepts simple relative ticketsPath
scenarios.when: the project config is loaded with ticketsPath set to 'docs/CRs'
scenarios.then: the configuration is accepted and the tickets directory resolves correctly

scenarios.title: Config accepts absolute ticketsPath
scenarios.when: the project config is loaded with ticketsPath set to '/var/mdt/tickets'
scenarios.then: the configuration is accepted and the tickets directory resolves to the absolute path

scenarios.title: Symlink followed when allowed and target is within ticketDir
scenarios.given: a project with allowSymlinks=true and a symlink inside ticketDir pointing to another file within ticketDir
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent for the symlinked subdocument name
scenarios.then: the response status is 200 and the symlink target content is returned

scenarios.title: Path traversal in subDocName is blocked
scenarios.given: a project with CR MDT-151 exists with a subdocument named 'architecture'
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent to /api/projects/{id}/crs/MDT-151/subdocuments/..%2Fsomething
scenarios.then: the response status is 404, no file outside ticketDir is read, and the response body matches a legitimate SubDocument not found response exactly

scenarios.title: Literal dotdot path traversal is blocked
scenarios.given: a project with CR MDT-151 exists
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent to /api/projects/{id}/crs/MDT-151/subdocuments/../something
scenarios.then: the response status is 404, no file outside ticketDir is read, and the response body matches a legitimate SubDocument not found response exactly

scenarios.title: Symlink inside ticketDir is not followed by default
scenarios.given: a project with a symlink inside the ticketDir pointing to an external file
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent for the symlinked subdocument name without allowSymlinks config
scenarios.then: the response status is 404, the symlink target is not read, and the response body matches a legitimate SubDocument not found response exactly

scenarios.title: Symlink rejected when target resolves outside ticketDir
scenarios.given: a project with allowSymlinks=true and a symlink inside ticketDir pointing to a file outside ticketDir
scenarios.when: a GET request is sent for the symlinked subdocument name
scenarios.then: the response status is 404, the external file is not read, and the response body matches a legitimate SubDocument not found response exactly

scenarios.title: Config rejects system root as ticketsPath
scenarios.when: the project config is loaded with ticketsPath set to '/etc'
scenarios.then: the configuration is rejected with error: Choose a subfolder, not a system root directory.

obligations.title: Wire ProjectValidator.validateTicketsPath() into server-side config load path (ProjectConfigService or project initialization) to reject traversal at load time
Add symlink detection and containment in SubdocumentService - default deny, opt-in allow via allowSymlinks config with realpathSync containment check on resolved target
Ensure path validation adds less than 1ms overhead - use synchronous fs ops only when needed (lstatSync for symlink check, not on every request)
Existing subdocument API behavior preserved - same endpoints, same response shape for valid requests; all existing tests continue to pass

Replace segment-count-only check in isSupportedSubdocumentPath() with whitelist validation - reject '..' segments, null bytes, double encoding, unicode slashes, whitespace-only, and length >255 at input validation before path resolution
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md: isSupportedSubdocumentPath only validates segment count

obligations.title: Add path containment validation to SubdocumentService.resolvePath() - reject any resolved path outside ticketDir, following the DocumentService pattern
obligations.sourceRef: assess.md: SubdocumentService path resolution mismatch, DocumentService proven pattern

obligations.title: Create security-focused unit tests for SubdocumentService (path traversal, encoding, null bytes, literal dotdot, length) and integration tests for API surface, plus config validation tests
System path blocklist for absolute ticketsPath

artifacts.sourceRef: assess.md: Structural Fit - single owner of subdoc path resolution
assess.md: server-side config load needs validation
assess.md: existing path utilities, potential containment helper location
assess.md: builds ticketDir, upstream of resolution
assess.md: has isPathWithinProject pattern to reference
assess.md: proven path-traversal defense pattern to follow
CR Section 5: Integration test for API surface
